name: ovld

modules.name: ovld.utils
documentation: Miscellaneous utilities.

functions.name: ovld.utils.keyword_decorator
documentation: Wrap a decorator to optionally takes keyword arguments.

classes.name: ovld.utils.Named

methods.name: ovld.utils.Named.__init__
documentation:
Construct a named object.

Arguments:
    name: The name of this object.

methods.name: ovld.utils.Named.__repr__
documentation: Return the object's name.

A named object.

This class can be used to construct objects with a name that will be used
for the string representation.

functions.name: ovld.mro.typeorder
documentation:
Order relation between two types.

Returns a member of the Order enum.

* typeorder(t1, t2) is Order.LESS   if t2 is more general than t1
* typeorder(t1, t2) is Order.SAME   if t1 is equal to t2
* typeorder(t1, t2) is Order.MORE   if t1 is more general than t2
* typeorder(t1, t2) is Order.NONE   if they cannot be compared

functions.name: ovld.mro.subclasscheck
documentation: Check whether t1 is a "subclass" of t2.

functions.name: ovld.types.class_check
documentation:
Return a class with a subclassing relation defined by condition.

For example, a dataclass is a subclass of `class_check(dataclasses.is_dataclass)`,
and a class which name starts with "X" is a subclass of
`class_check(lambda cls: cls.__name__.startswith("X"))`.

Arguments:
    condition: A function that takes a class as an argument and returns
        True or False depending on whether it matches some condition.

functions.name: ovld.types.parametrized_class_check
documentation:
Return a parametrized class checker.

In essence, parametrized_class_check(fn)[X] will call fn(cls, X) in order
to check whether cls matches the condition defined by fn and X.

Arguments:
    fn: A function that takes a class and one or more additional arguments,
        and returns True or False depending on whether the class matches.

functions.name: ovld.types.Exactly
documentation: Match the class but not its subclasses.

functions.name: ovld.types.StrictSubclass
documentation: Match subclasses but not the base class.

functions.name: ovld.types.HasMethod
documentation: Match classes that have a specific method.

classes.name: ovld.types.All
documentation:
All is the empty/void/bottom type -- it acts as a subtype of all types.

It is basically the opposite of Any: nothing is an instance of All. The main
place you want to use All is as a wildcard in contravariant settings, e.g.
all 2-argument functions are instances of Callable[[All, All], Any] because
the arguments are contravariant.

classes.name: ovld.types.Whatever
documentation:
This type is a superclass and a subclass of everything.

It's not a coherent type, more like a convenience.

It'll match anything anywhere, so you can write e.g.
Callable[[Whatever, Whatever], Whatever] to match any function of
two arguments. Any only works in covariant settings, and All only
works in contravariant settings.

classes.name: ovld.types.Deferred
documentation:
Represent a class from an external module without importing it.

For instance, `Deferred["numpy.ndarray"]` matches instances of
numpy.ndarray, but it does not import numpy. When tested against a
class, if the first part of class's `__module__` is `numpy`, then
we do get the class and perform a normal issubclass check.

If the module is already loaded, `Deferred` returns the class directly.

Arguments:
    ref: A string starting with a module name representing the path
        to import a class.

classes.name: ovld.typemap.TypeMap

methods.name: ovld.typemap.TypeMap.register
documentation: Register a handler for the given object type.

methods.name: ovld.typemap.TypeMap.__missing__
documentation:
Get the handler for the given type.

The result is cached so that the normal dict getitem will find it
the next time getitem is called.

Represents a mapping from types to handlers.

The mro of a type is considered when getting the handler, so setting the
[object] key creates a default for all objects.

typemap[some_type] returns a tuple of a handler and a "level" that
represents the distance from the handler to the type `object`. Essentially,
the level is the index of the type for which the handler was registered
in the mro of `some_type`. So for example, `object` has level 0, a class
that inherits directly from `object` has level 1, and so on.

classes.name: ovld.typemap.MultiTypeMap

methods.name: ovld.typemap.MultiTypeMap.register
documentation:
Register a handler for a tuple of argument types.

Arguments:
    sig: A Signature object.
    handler: A function to handle the tuple.

Represents a mapping from tuples of types to handlers.

The mro is taken into account to find a match. If multiple registered
handlers match the tuple of types that's given, if one of the handlers is
more specific than every other handler, that handler is returned.
Otherwise, the resolution is considered ambiguous and an error is raised.

Handler A, registered for types (A1, A2, ..., An), is more specific than
handler B, registered for types (B1, B2, ..., Bn), if there exists n such
that An is more specific than Bn, and for all n, either An == Bn or An is
more specific than Bn. An is more specific than Bn if An is a direct or
indirect subclass of Bn.

In other words, [int, object] is more specific than [object, object] and
less specific than [int, int], but it is neither less specific nor more
specific than [object, int] (which means there is an ambiguity).

modules.name: ovld.core
documentation: Utilities to overload functions for multiple types.

functions.name: ovld.core.is_ovld
documentation: Return whether the argument is an ovld function/method.

functions.name: ovld.core.to_ovld
documentation: Return whether the argument is an ovld function/method.

functions.name: ovld.core.extend_super
documentation:
Declare that this method extends the super method with more types.

This produces an ovld using the superclass method of the same name,
plus this definition and others with the same name.

functions.name: ovld.core.ovld
documentation:
Overload a function.

Overloading is based on the function name.

The decorated function should have one parameter annotated with a type.
Any parameter can be annotated, but only one, and every overloading of a
function should annotate the same parameter.

The decorator optionally takes keyword arguments, *only* on the first
use.

Arguments:
    fn: The function to register.
    priority: The priority of the function in the resolution order.
    fresh: Whether to create a new ovld or try to reuse an existing one.
    mixins: A list of Ovld instances that contribute functions to this
        Ovld.
    name: Optional name for the Ovld. If not provided, it will be
        gotten automatically from the first registered function or
        dispatch.
    linkback: Whether to keep a pointer in the parent mixins to this
        ovld so that updates can be propagated. (default: False)

classes.name: ovld.core.Ovld

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.__init__
documentation: Initialize an Ovld.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.rename
documentation: Rename this Ovld.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld._set_attrs_from
documentation: Inherit relevant attributes from the function.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.compile
documentation:
Finalize this overload.

This will populate the type maps and replace the functions decorated
with _compile_first (__call__, __get__, etc.) with versions that assume
the ovld has been compiled.

This will also lock this ovld's parent mixins to prevent their
modification.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.resolve
documentation: Find the correct method to call for the given arguments.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.register_signature
documentation: Register a function for the given signature.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.register
documentation: Register a function.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld._register
documentation: Register a function.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.unregister
documentation: Unregister a function.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of this Ovld.

New functions can be registered to the copy without affecting the
original.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.variant
documentation:
Decorator to create a variant of this Ovld.

New functions can be registered to the variant without affecting the
original.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.__call__
documentation:
Call the overloaded function.

This should be replaced by an auto-generated function.

methods.name: ovld.core.Ovld.next
documentation: Call the next matching method after the caller, in terms of priority or specificity.

Overloaded function.

A function can be added with the `register` method. One of its parameters
should be annotated with a type, but only one, and every registered
function should annotate the same parameter.

Arguments:
    mixins: A list of Ovld instances that contribute functions to this
        Ovld.
    name: Optional name for the Ovld. If not provided, it will be
        gotten automatically from the first registered function or
        dispatch.
    linkback: Whether to keep a pointer in the parent mixins to this
        ovld so that updates can be propagated. (default: False)
    allow_replacement: Allow replacing a method by another with the
        same signature. (default: True)

classes.name: ovld.core.ovld_cls_dict
documentation:
A dict for use with OvldMC.__prepare__.

Setting a key that already corresponds to an Olvd extends that Ovld.

classes.name: ovld.core.OvldMC

methods.name: ovld.core.OvldMC.create_subclass
documentation: Create a new subclass with the given extra bases.

Metaclass that allows overloading.

A class which uses this metaclass can define multiple functions with
the same name and different type signatures.

classes.name: ovld.core.OvldBase
documentation: Base class that allows overloading of methods.

functions.name: ovld.recode.rename_function
documentation: Create a copy of the function with a different name.

functions.name: ovld.recode.adapt_function
documentation: Create a copy of the function with a different name.
